checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md created with all three acceptance criteria: stable-contract declaration ('we treat them as a public API contract'), staged deprecation policy with grace period serving old IDs, changelog expectations section. Linked from README.md and docs/API.md. No source code changed — correct for a docs-only deliverable.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only 3 documentation files touched: new docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (77 lines of substantive content), plus one-line link additions to README.md and docs/API.md. No unrelated churn, no source edits, exactly the files the plan called for.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: Documentation is real, substantive prose: active provider table (25 entries), four-stage deprecation policy with headers and HTTP status codes, changelog expectations with migration notes, scope caveats. No tests modified; verify command is a genuine pre-existing pass, not gamed.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: Content is accurate, well-structured, and non-normative as specified. Provider IDs are realistic. Deprecation stages are clearly bounded with timelines. Scope section explicitly disclaims voice-level stability and pricing, avoiding overpromise. No security or correctness risk — safe to advance to operator PR.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence
